title: Gossamer Venezuelan Tofu Bowl
description: A light, salty bowl of pillowy tofu tossed in a tangy apple cider vinegar and ferment-jang sauce, served over fluffy quinoa.
instructions: Press tofu for 20 minutes to remove excess moisture, then cube it.
In a bowl, whisk together soy sauce, apple cider vinegar, ferment-jang, coconut milk, and peanut butter until smooth.
Heat 3 tbsp oil in a skillet over medium heat, add minced garlic and grated ginger, sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add tofu cubes to the skillet, cook until golden brown on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Pour the sauce mixture over the tofu, stir to coat and let simmer for 3 minutes until sauce thickens slightly.
Meanwhile, cook 1 cup quinoa according to package instructions, fluff with a fork once ready.
Plate a bed of quinoa in each bowl, top with the sautéed tofu and sauce.
Garnish with sliced scallions and a squeeze of lime juice.
Serve immediately for a salty, pillowy, light Venezuelan tofu bowl.
